steps.title: Set up the expression
steps.content: There are 5 stations total. If $x$ stations use Experiment A (6 tsp each), then $5 - x$ stations use Experiment B (4 tsp each).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm C
steps.content:
• Choice A — $5x$: Treats $x$ as the total salt per station regardless of experiment, ignoring that Experiment A and Experiment B have different teaspoon amounts and that the $5 - x$ Experiment B stations each contribute 4 tsp. ✗
  • Choice B — $10x$: Adds $6 + 4 = 10$ and multiplies by $x$, incorrectly applying both per-station rates to the same $x$ stations and omitting the $4(5 - x)$ contribution from the Experiment B stations entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — $2x + 20$: Matches the simplified expression exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — $10x + 20$: Would only be correct if Experiment A required 10 tsp per station and Experiment B required 0 tsp; incorrect. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
